data.title: SA Asks: What's the best gold play right now for investors?
data.description:
The price of gold hit a record high again this past week amid increased geopolitical tensions and ongoing economic uncertainty.  

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Andrew Hecht, Eliana Scialabba, and Taylor Dart what they saw as the best gold play for investors right now.

Andrew Hecht: The trend is your friend, and it's higher going into 2026. Moreover, gold's ascent reflects the decline in fiat currency values and purchasing power. SPDR Gold Shares ETF (GLD), iShares Gold Trust ETF (IAU), and GraniteShares Gold Trust ETF (BAR) are pure gold plays, as the three ETFs hold physical gold bullion. Gold's bull market is entering its 27th year, and buying on price weakness has been optimal. I expect that trend to continue in 2026. 

Eliana Scialabba: For gold, I’m clearly leaning toward Newmont (NEM) and Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM). What really stands out for me is how both companies are taking advantage of the gold cycle and actually converting higher prices into cash flow. This is not just about gold going up, but about execution, balance sheet discipline, and capital allocation, and in that sense, both names are doing a very solid job.

In past cycles, many gold miners failed to monetize higher prices. This time feels different. NEM and AEM are showing much better financial structures, more controlled capex, and a clearer focus on free cash flow generation, which is exactly what I want at this stage.

I also find Barrick Gold (B) increasingly interesting. Recent news around the possible IPO and progress toward resolving the Mali situation have helped reduce a major uncertainty. The company still offers strong leverage to gold prices, and if geopolitical risk continues to ease, the risk/reward profile looks more attractive than it did before.

For diversified exposure, I like the iShares MSCI Global Gold Miners ETF (RING). It gives direct exposure to gold miners globally, without being linked to physical gold, and allows investors to capture the operational leverage that is working well in this part of the cycle.

Taylor Dart: With the price of gold surging in Q4, several names are bumping up against their estimated fair values, with a few sitting above fair value and at levels of extreme over-extension short-term, such as Hycroft Mining (HYMC) above $27.00, Kinross Gold (KGC) above $29.00, and G-Mining Ventures (GMINF) above $31.00, as well as several others highlighted here on short-term sell signals. 

However, there is still value to be had in the sector for those willing to dig through the rubble, with one name being Alkane Resources (ALKEF) (ALK:CA), sporting an enterprise value of just over $1 billion and set to exit 2025 with ~$120 million in net cash.

Despite a Tier-1 only production profile with operations in Sweden and Australia (three mines in total), with its Bjorkdal Mine benefiting from extremely low royalty rates (1/50th the royalty rates of many West African countries) and low-cost hydropower, this ~180,000-ounce-per-annum gold and antimony producer trades at just over 6x calendar year 2026 enterprise value to free cash flow estimates at a share price of $0.85 using a conservative $3,900/oz gold price. At a $4,400/oz gold price, this figure improves to just over 4x calendar year 2026 EV/FCF estimates. 

In addition, Alkane is busy undertaking an aggressive drill program at its new True Blue discovery in Australia, which sits barely 20 kilometers from Agnico Eagle's (AEM) high-grade Fosterville Mine, with recent drill hits including 0.47 meters at 18.5 ounces per tonne of gold and 20.5% antimony, well above its average reserve grades. 

So, with multiple organic growth opportunities across the portfolio, the potential to bring True Blue into the Costerfield Mine plan later this decade to spike feed grades, and growing production at Tomingley, I continue to view Alkane as the most attractive way to play the gold sector today.

data.title: Insider Trades: Nike, Oracle, Broadcom among notable names this week
data.description:
Companies including Nike (NKE), CrowdStrike (CRWD) and Oracle (ORCL) saw significant insider activity in the holiday-shortened week, as markets were closed on Thursday for Christmas celebrations. The following is a list of transactions that took place between December 22 and December 28.

  • Tim Cook bought a stake worth nearly $3M in Nike (NKE) as the director of the company. He bought 50,000 shares for an average price of $58.97 each, increasing his holdings to 105,480 shares in the firm. The stock jumped after Cook’s purchase on Wednesday and closed the day 4.64% higher. Meanwhile, Robert Swan, who’s also a director at the company, purchased 8,691 shares for $57.54 each for a total value of $500,080. Swan now holds 44,873 shares of the firm.
  • Broadcom (AVGO) Harry You added 1,000 company shares to his portfolio for $325.129 each for a total value of $325,129. You now holds 36,602 shares of the firm.
  • Meanwhile, on the sale side, Broadcom’s (AVGO) CEO Hock Tan sold company shares worth $42.38M. He let go of 130,000 shares for $326.02 each. Tan continues to hold 1.08M shares following the sale.
  • Steven Gomo, director at Micron Technology (MU), sold 5,000 shares for $263.63 each for a total value of $1.32M. Gomo holds 19,139 Micron shares following the sale.
  • Several CrowdStrike (CRWD) insiders divested their holdings this week, cumulatively worth around $38.81M. Of these, CEO George Kurtz made the biggest  sale for about $8.45M. He sold 17,550 shares in the price range of $475.57 to $485.12, reducing his holdings to 2.19M shares.
  • Oracle’s (ORCL) chief executive officer, Clayton Magouyrk, let go of 10,000 shares for $192.52 apiece for a total of $1.93M. Magouyrk now holds 144,030 shares in the firm.
  • FuelCell Energy (FCEL) director Betsy Bingham sold 8,608 shares for $8.52 each for a total value of $73,371.

data.title: China’s industrial profits drop for the second consecutive month in November
data.description:
Profits at China's industrial firms fell 13.1% YoY in November following a 5.5% YoY drop in October and marking the steepest decline in more than a year, according to data released by the country’s National Bureau of Statistics on Saturday.

Chinese industrial firms last saw such a sharp earnings decline in September 2024 when their industrial profits slid 27.1% due to factors such as inadequate demand and a steep decline in producer prices.

In September, China recorded a 21.6% rise in industrial profits, marking the second straight month of gains. Bloomberg Economics had projected a 15% drop in industrial profits for October.

However, for the first 11 months of 2025, the country's industrial profits have risen 0.1% YoY, a slowdown from 1.9% growth in Jan.–Oct. Among notable industries, profits in the coal mining and washing industry fell 47.3%, while the oil and gas extraction industry recorded a 13.6% drop.

China-related ETFs: (FXI), (KWEB), (CQQQ), (MCHI), (ASHR), (YINN), (TDF), (CHIQ), (GXC), (EWH), (KBA), (YANG), (CXSE), (CAF), (CWEB), (PGJ), (KURE).

Currency: (CNY:USD)

data.title: Airlines cancel over 1,000 flights as winter storm hits U.S. Northeast
data.description:
Airlines in the United States have canceled or delayed thousands of flights as a severe winter storm started to barrel down on the country’s Northeast early on Saturday, disrupting a busy travel period between Christmas and New Year’s.

While over 1,700 commercial flights within, into, or out of the U.S. were canceled on Friday, more than 9,200 were delayed, according to data from FlightAware, which monitors flight delays and cancellations worldwide.

Saturday’s cancellations and delays reached 657 and 2,448, respectively. The three major airports serving the New York City area, John F. Kennedy International, Newark Liberty International, and LaGuardia, posted snow warnings on the social media site X, as the trio contributed to most of the flight cancellations on Friday.

American Airlines (AAL), United Airlines (UAL), and JetBlue Airways (JBLU) have waived change fees normally charged for rebooking customers whose flights were impacted by weather-related disruptions, Reuters reported, citing company representatives.

On Friday, New York Governor Kathy Hochul and New Jersey Acting Governor Tahesha Way issued emergency declarations in response to the incoming winter storm. According to industry lobby group Airlines for America, U.S. carriers expect to fly a record 52.6M Americans between Dec. 19 and Jan. 5, averaging 2.9M travelers daily, with Apr. 28 being among the busiest days for air travel.

data.title: China proposes draft rules to regulate human-like AI systems
data.description:
China's internet regulator on Saturday issued draft rules that would regulate the use of artificial intelligence systems that interact with humans and display human-like traits and behavior.

Titled "Interim Measures for the Administration of Anthropomorphic Interactive Services Using Artificial Intelligence," the Cyberspace Administration of China ((CAC)) said it was opening up the draft rules for public comment until January 25, 2026. 

The regulator said the rules would apply to products and services that use AI with "simulated human personality traits" and engage "in emotional interaction," or what it called "anthropomorphic interactive services."

The proposed regulations include making it clear to users that they are interacting with an AI rather than a real person through timely pop-up windows, asking users to take a break at two-hour intervals during continuous use of a human-like AI system, and assessing users' emotions and identifying if they have become dependent or addicted to the AI. 

The rules also ask providers of human-like AI systems to establish an emergency response mechanism under which, if a user expresses thoughts about suicide or self-harm, then a human should take over the interaction from the AI.

According to the CAC, these draft rules will "promote the healthy development and standardized application of artificial intelligence-based anthropomorphic interactive services, safeguard national security and public interests, and protect the legitimate rights and interests of citizens, legal persons and other organizations."

The move comes at a time when China, after the U.S., leads the world in AI technology and underscores the Asian nation's efforts to shape AI regulation.

Here are some China-focused exchange-traded funds: (MCHI), (KWEB), (PGJ), (CQQQ), (FXI), (GXC), (FLCH), (CNYA), (ASHR), and (YINN). 

And here are some AI- and robotics-focused ETFs: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), and (CHAT).

data.title: UnitedHealth, Humana lead in excessive Medicare drug refill costs: study
data.description:
Early prescription refills by U.S. pharmacies cost Medicare and patients $3B in 2021–2023, with mail-order pharmacies run by UnitedHealth (UNH) and Humana (HUM) leading the cost overruns, according to a study conducted by the Wall Street Journal.

The study, based on details of each prescription sent to more than 50M Medicare beneficiaries between 2021 and 2023, found that mail-order pharmacies accounted for 37% of the excess dispensing even though they filled only 9% of Medicare prescriptions.

Some insurers require their customers to use 75% of a prior supply, or day 68 for a 90-day refill, before sending prescription refills. However, according to the WSJ, UnitedHealth’s (UNH) mail-order pharmacies sent refills sooner than the 68-day threshold 11% of the time, nearly nine times faster than other Medicare pharmacies.

Over the three years, UnitedHealth (UNH) delivered $142 worth of excess medications per Medicare patient, the most among any major pharmacy operator, while Humana (HUM) came second with $93 worth of extra drugs per recipient.

CVS Health’s (CVS) mail-order pharmacy and Cigna’s (CI) Express Scripts cost Medicare $45 and $44 worth of extra medications per patient, respectively.

While Medicare requires drug plans that administer the federal health program to set limits on early prescription refills, the government relaxed those rules in 2020 as part of its pandemic response, allowing early refills for some time.

Both UnitedHealth (UNH) and Humana (HUM) noted that since 2023, when COVID-era emergency rules ended, they have rejected more early refills than the period considered in the study.

“The data does not reflect current practices,” a spokesperson for UnitedHealth (UNH) said, adding that the company denied up to five times as many early refills in 2024 compared to 2021 and 2022.

Humana (HUM) argued that its current practices “strike the right balance, allowing sufficient time to fill and ship prescriptions and supporting medication adherence without encouraging stockpiling.”

data.title: Brookfield’s Center Parcs draws U.K. pension funds in refinancing push: report
data.description:
Some of the leading public sector pension funds in the U.K. are eyeing a stake in Center Parcs, one of the well-known leisure brands in Britain, as part of a refinancing process initiated by its Canadian owner, Brookfield Asset Management (BAM), Sky News reported on Saturday.

According to sources, the Greater Manchester Pension Fund, the London-based Local Pension Partnership, and the Edinburgh-based Lothian Pension Scheme are among the parties involved in talks with Brookfield (BAM) to acquire a 15% to 20% stake in Center Parcs.

The process is set to conclude in Q1 2026, resulting in approximately £4.5B ($6.1B) of valuation for Center Parcs, which attracts millions of visitors annually through its five U.K. sites and a newly established property in Ireland.

With more than £30B in assets under management and serving over 430,000 members, Greater Manchester Pension Fund is the U.K.’s biggest local government pension scheme.

Brookfield (BAM) is expected to remain the majority owner of the leisure group after the refinancing process, which could also see its existing shareholder, the Chinese sovereign wealth fund China Investment Corporation, injecting additional capital into the company.

No deal has been finalized, and some of the retirement schemes involved in the talks may eventually opt out of an agreement, the sources said.

data.title: Oil prices slide on renewed hopes for Russia-Ukraine peace deal
data.description:
Crude oil futures squeezed out small gains for the week while losing ground Friday in light post-holiday trading amid efforts to broker an end to the Russia-Ukraine war.

Ukraine's President Zelenskiy said he plans to meet with President Trump on Sunday to discuss U.S. efforts to secure a peace deal between his country and Russia, with reports indicating some possible progress; an agreement could lead to an easing of Western sanctions on Russian petroleum exports.

The Trump administration has continued to tighten its stance on Venezuelan oil, with stepped-up interdictions and tougher enforcement increasing risks of temporary export disruptions, and the U.S. Coast Guard and military are assembling more manpower and weapons to catch a runaway oil tanker linked to Iran and Venezuela.

The Venezuelan blockade and heavy travel demand are positives for oil, while negatives are elevated global oil storage and slight progress on Russia-Ukraine peace talks, BOK Financial senior VP of trading Dennis Kissler said in a note.

"Venezuelan oil exports have been less than 400,000 bbl/day the past couple of months, which is half what they were exporting last year," Kissler wrote. "While the U.S. blockade has turned the pressure up on Venezuela, the global impact to crude prices looks minimal at this time."

While supply disruptions have helped boost oil prices in recent sessions from nearly five-year lows last week, they remain on track for their steepest annual decline since 2020, with Brent and WTI crude down 19% and 21% Y/Y, respectively, as rising global crude production caused concerns of a glut heading into next year.

On Friday, front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for February finished -2.7% to $56.74/bbl and front-month February Brent crude (CO1:COM) ended -2.6% to $60.64/bbl; for the full week, Nymex crude edged 0.4% higher and Brent crude closed up 0.3%.

U.S. natural gas futures rose Friday after weather models over the Christmas break added more cold to the outlook; front-month Nymex natgas (NG1:COM) for January delivery closed +2.9% to $4.366/MMBtu, wrapping up a 9.6% rally for the week.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE), finished the holiday-shortened week up 0.15%.

data.title: Silver soars to hit another record high, gold extends rally
data.description:
The run-up in precious metals broke new ground Friday, as front-month silver futures surged 7.7% to nearly $76.49/oz, extending a record run that has more than doubled prices this year, while gold futures also hit records, rising 1.1% to $4,529.10/oz.

One factor boosting safe-haven precious metals has been escalating geopolitical tensions, as the U.S. struck ISIS targets in Nigeria in a Christmas Day bombing and is chasing a runaway oil tanker in the Atlantic, part of the Trump administration's blockade of sanctioned oil tankers entering and exiting Venezuelan waters.

Also, hostilities between Russia and Ukraine remain elevated despite peace negotiations, and Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said this week that Israel was watching Hamas, Hezbollah, and Iran as they rearm and would act if necessary.

The U.S. dollar index posted a weekly decline, enhancing the appeal of dollar-priced gold for overseas buyers.

"Expectations for further Fed easing in 2026, a weak dollar and heightened geopolitical tensions are driving volatility in thin markets," Zaner Metals VP and senior metals strategist Peter Grant said in a note. "While there is some risk of profit-taking before the year-end, the trend remains strong."

For the week, front-month Comex silver (XAGUSD:CUR) for December delivery ended +14.4% to $76.486/bbl this week, and front-month Comex December gold (XAUUSD:CUR) closed +3.8% to $4,529.10/oz; for the full year, silver has surged 164% and gold has gained 72%.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: ACM Research is the top performing semiconductor materials stock YTD
data.description:
Information technology index was the second-best-performing sector in 2025, as it rose +25% YTD, while its accompanying State Street Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) is +26.14%.

As we look at the subsectors of the IT index, below is a list of the top 10 semiconductor materials stocks ranked by their YTD performance. The list includes Quant ratings for each stock.

The list is topped by ACM Research (ACMR), with a YTD performance of +165.56%. Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) and KLA Corporation (KLAC) follow as strong performers, with YTD gains of +145.51% and +102.66% respectively, all three exceeding 100% YTD performance.

Other notable top performers include Nova (NVMI) with a 70.77% YTD gain, which also carries the highest Quant rating among the top stocks at Buy 3.95. Daqo New Energy (DQ) and Applied Materials (AMAT) round out the next tier of performers with gains exceeding 60%.

Here is the list:

  1. ACM Research (ACMR), YTD perf: +165.56%, Quant Rating: Hold 2.99
